description: Instead of fixed routing strategies (like binary or top-k), employ reinforcement learning to dynamically adjust expert selection for each token based on past performance and model state. The RL agent learns to optimize routing based on a reward function that balances throughput, latency, and capacity utilization.
novelty: Moves beyond static routing to a dynamic, adaptive approach guided by learning, potentially overcoming the limitations of fixed routing schemes and improving capacity utilization.
challenges: Training the RL agent effectively, ensuring stability during training, and designing a suitable reward function that accurately reflects the desired performance characteristics.

description: Structure MoE layers hierarchically, with lower layers focusing on coarse-grained routing and higher layers refining the selection. Implement sparse activation techniques at each layer to reduce the number of active experts, mitigating communication overhead and improving convergence speed.
novelty: Combines hierarchical routing with sparse activation to create a more efficient and scalable MoE architecture, addressing both routing complexity and communication costs.
challenges: Designing the hierarchical structure effectively, determining the optimal granularity of the hierarchy, and implementing efficient sparse activation mechanisms.

approach: Communication-Aware Tensor Parallelism with Dynamic Partitioning
description: Go beyond static tensor parallelism by dynamically partitioning tensors based on communication patterns during training. Utilize techniques like graph neural networks to analyze communication bottlenecks and adjust tensor placement to minimize inter-device communication.
novelty: Moves away from fixed tensor partitioning to a dynamic approach that adapts to the communication landscape of the model, potentially reducing communication overhead significantly.
challenges: Developing efficient algorithms for dynamic tensor partitioning, maintaining model consistency during partitioning changes, and ensuring scalability to very large models.

approach: Neural Architecture Search (NAS) for MoE Routing
description: Employ NAS to automatically discover optimal routing architectures for MoE layers. The NAS algorithm explores different routing topologies and activation functions, searching for configurations that maximize performance and efficiency.
novelty: Automates the design of MoE routing strategies, potentially uncovering novel architectures that outperform hand-designed approaches.
challenges: Computational cost of NAS, ensuring the search space is sufficiently diverse, and validating the performance of discovered routing architectures on real-world tasks.

description: Combine tensor, pipeline, and MoE parallelism, but with a task-specific decomposition strategy. Analyze the computational workload of different layers and tasks and assign them to the most appropriate parallelism strategy based on their characteristics.
novelty: Moves beyond a one-size-fits-all approach to parallelism, tailoring the strategy to the specific needs of different parts of the model.
challenges: Developing effective methods for task-specific decomposition, ensuring seamless integration of different parallelism strategies, and managing the complexity of the hybrid architecture.
